title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (March 11)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington homes sold include a Colonial built in 1900 for $555,000 and a ranch from 1968 for $550,000.
Andover sales feature a 7,157 sq. ft. contemporary home built in 1993, sold for $1,778,500.
Boston had the most expensive sale with a row-middle property on Chestnut St. for $22,000,000.

title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the game-winning goal for Boston Bruins against the Los Angeles Kings in overtime.
After scoring, McAvoy, who was hit in the face during the game, required further dental treatment.
McAvoy's performance highlights his toughness and potential leadership role in the Bruins' team dynamic.

title: Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected in the second quarter against the Spurs after two technical fouls.
Brown criticized NBA officials on social media post-ejection, referencing perceived fouls and officiating issues.
His absence was noted as the Celtics, already missing two players, lost 125-116 to the Spurs.

title: Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Angel Alvarez, 68, sentenced to life in prison for stabbing Margarita Morehead, 59, in her Chelsea apartment.
Alvarez pleaded guilty to second-degree murder and other charges, changing his initial not guilty plea from September 2023.
Alvarez violated a restraining order and later evaded police before turning himself in after multiple admissions of guilt.

title: MBTA police seeking suspect who allegedly spat on, hit rider
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: MBTA Transit Police seek help identifying suspect in Feb. 27 assault at Prudential station.
Suspect allegedly spat on and hit Green Line rider multiple times with a closed fist.
Suspect is described as wearing black, with a hoodie, short hair, beard, and neck and hand tattoos.

title: Maine man allegedly shot and killed girlfriend; pair worked together at ski resort
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Austin Doucette, 24, was arrested for allegedly killing his girlfriend Makayla Rose DeSantis, 23, in Carrabassett Valley, Maine.
Makayla DeSantis was shot at a residence connected to Sugarloaf Mountain Ski Resort, where the couple worked.
Doucette is held without bail as Maine State Police continue investigating the incident involving the seven-year couple.

title: Patriots reportedly remain interested in A.J. Brown trade after signing Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots signed Romeo Doubs to a four-year contract worth up to $80 million.
The Patriots are still interested in trading for Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown despite signing Doubs.
Philadelphia reportedly demands a 2027 first-round and 2026 second-round pick for A.J. Brown in trade talks.

title: She’s running the Boston Marathon to support breast cancer patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sydney Weisman, 24, from Fort Lauderdale, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for breast cancer support.
Weisman is running with Team Ellie to honor her mother's fight against breast cancer.
The Ellie Fund provides transportation, meals, childcare, and support to breast cancer patients and families.

title: 3 Stop & Shop locations in Boston, Reading receive bomb threats, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police responded to bomb threats at Stop & Shop locations in Jamaica Plain and Allston on Monday.
A similar bomb threat was reported at a Stop & Shop in Reading, prompting evacuation and investigation.
Police are investigating whether the three threats, all received within minutes, are related.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots WR Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots signed receiver Romeo Doubs to a four-year, $68 million contract, potentially reaching $80 million.
Doubs recorded 724 receiving yards and six touchdowns with the Packers in the 2025 season.
Doubs is known for his playoff performances, achieving over 100 yards in two of four games.
